description_en: The Lord's Supper as the central contested locus among all three traditions
How Christ is present in/with/under the elements: real presence, spiritual presence, memorial, figurative
Who receives and in what form: sub utraque specie, infant communion, preparation requirements
Whether the Eucharist is a sacrifice (oblatio) or thanksgiving (eucharistia); Mass as re-presentation vs. memorial
Devotional practices: adoration, elevation, reservation, procession — and their theological implications
Role and effect of Christ's words in the celebration; consecration theology; relationship between Word and element
The nature of the church and its ministry — the locus where institutional differences are sharpest
What the church IS: visible/invisible distinction, body of Christ, congregation of saints, marks of the church
Who leads and by what authority: ordained ministry, universal priesthood, episcopal vs. presbyterial polity
Legitimacy of ministerial authority: succession of doctrine vs. succession of office; Waldensian ordination (Unity); Venice ordinations (Utraquist)
Internal governance: excommunication, fraternal admonition, moral supervision — esp. distinctive in Unity practice
Political authority and church autonomy: two kingdoms doctrine, cuius regio, religious liberty
Veneration of Mary and the saints; intercession; the Reformation re-evaluation of saintly mediation; the editorial fate of Marian hymnody under confessional revision
Justification and its ethical consequences — where Lutheran and Czech Reformation accents diverge most
How salvation is received: sola fide, imputation of alien righteousness, forensic vs. effective justification
How the justified life is lived: regeneration, new obedience, fruits of faith — Unity's distinctive emphasis
Relationship between belief and practice: merit, charity, disciplina vitae — the practical divergence point
Process of reconciliation: contrition, confession, absolution — different penitential practices across traditions
Final destiny and present hope: resurrection, eternal life, judgment, kingdom of God — as expressed in hymns
Approaches to biblical authority and the role of non-scriptural sources
Status and sufficiency of Scripture: sola scriptura, verbum Dei, inspiration, perspicuity
Role of Church Fathers: consensus patrum, tradition as interpretive guide vs. as binding authority
Status of confessional documents: norma normata, binding force, relationship to Scripture
How Scripture is to be interpreted: sensus literalis, analogia fidei, scriptura sui interpres, Christological centre
Language as theological medium: bibličtina, vernacular principle, Latin persistence, Slovak emergence through CS
